repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
resolves #39424

## Problem:
When `:!` writes shell output to a buffer, write_output() splits on `\r`, `\n`,
and `\r\n`, replacing the terminator byte with NUL. For a binary-mode buffer
this is wrong: `\r` should be preserved verbatim, not treated as a line
terminator. This wrong behavior causes a file like `\r\n` round-trips through
`:%!cat` to `\n`.

This was masked when 'shelltemp' was enabled, because output went through a temp
file and the regular file I/O path handled binary-mode correctly. Switching the
default to 'noshelltemp' exposed the bug, since output is now piped directly
into write_output().

## Solution:
In `write_output()`, skip the `\r` and `\r\n` splits for a binary-mode buffer;
only split on `\n`.

comment_data.body:
would love to hear a core developer opinion on this one, do you think it is better to use `get_fileformat(buf) == EOL_DOS` here instead? This is only an issue on windows when writing output from `pwsh` as it returns string with `\r\n` as EOL, see failing test without this `if`:

https://github.com/neovim/neovim/actions/runs/25250799421/job/74042474681?pr=39558#step:7:11148
here we have the same for old macos versions (or old tools for macos?), not sure if this is even an real case out there.
yeah it's not relevant for macos anymore, except that some ancient *file* on macos in theory might have CR lineendings.
not sure, it would help if this PR had a minimal Problem/Solution so that I know what I'm reviewing :)

seems misleading (we are testing `:!` read, not write), is this a more accurate description:


```suggestion
  it('preserves newlines (including CR) in binary-mode buffer #39424', function()
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -567,6 +570,16 @@ end)
 describe('shell :!', function()
   before_each(clear)
 
+  it('write in binary mode should not remove CR #39424', function()

comment_data.body:
> This is only an issue on windows when writing output from `pwsh` as it returns string with `\r\n` as EOL

? https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/39424 shows a non-powershell example

It's kind of confusing, we are reading the output of the shell command and writing it into the buffer, the fix was made in the write_output function. So I guess depending on how you look at it, it's a write or a read, but strictly speaking it's on the write part of the whole process. 

Don't really trying to argue here, I just also struggled initially how to fraze it. I will make the changes you suggested when I am not on my mobile phone.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -567,6 +570,16 @@ end)
 describe('shell :!', function()
   before_each(clear)
 
+  it('write in binary mode should not remove CR #39424', function()

comment_data.body:
> not sure, it would help if this PR had a minimal Problem/Solution so that I know what I'm reviewing :)

I will add it :)

> > This is only an issue on windows when writing output from `pwsh` as it returns string with `\r\n` as EOL
> 
> ? https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/39424 shows a non-powershell example

The patch provided in that issue (https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/39424#issuecomment-4322251177), is now enough to solve the issue for all cases, the URL above should only show where it would break with only that patch applied and should also show why it was made like that in the first place, because for pwsh we need that special case.

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -567,6 +570,16 @@ end)
 describe('shell :!', function()
   before_each(clear)
 
+  it('preserves newlines (including CR) in binary-mode buffer #39424', function()
+    local fname = 'Xbinaryfile'
+    os.remove(fname)

comment_data.body: feel free to just use `n.fn`. idk people like to define all these aliases up here, the reason `t` and `n` are 1 char is to avoid the need for this
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -6,9 +6,12 @@ local Screen = require('test.functional.ui.screen')
 
 local assert_alive = n.assert_alive
 local testprg = n.testprg
+local fn = n.fn

comment_data.body:
this looks a bit weird, is the NUL byte intended? I assume this is an unintended consequence of using `writefile({ '\r\n' }` which, because of Vim's legacy, Vim treats as the `\n` as a NUL byte.

I'm not sure if it matters for your test, but if it's not intended, you can instead just do:

    eq(0, fn.writefile('\r\n', fname, 'b'))

and that should write an actual CRLF. (Note this is a recent feature because of https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/39098 )
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -567,6 +570,16 @@ end)
 describe('shell :!', function()
   before_each(clear)
 
+  it('preserves newlines (including CR) in binary-mode buffer #39424', function()
+    local fname = 'Xbinaryfile'
+    os.remove(fname)
+    eq(0, fn.writefile({ '\r\n' }, fname, 'b'))
+    command('edit ++bin ' .. fname)
+    command('%!cat')
+    command('w')
+    eq('\r\0', read_file(fname))

comment_data.body: Isn't it better to use `t.write_file()`? That allows backporting.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -567,6 +570,16 @@ end)
 describe('shell :!', function()
   before_each(clear)
 
+  it('preserves newlines (including CR) in binary-mode buffer #39424', function()
+    local fname = 'Xbinaryfile'
+    os.remove(fname)
+    eq(0, fn.writefile({ '\r\n' }, fname, 'b'))
+    command('edit ++bin ' .. fname)
+    command('%!cat')
+    command('w')
+    eq('\r\0', read_file(fname))

comment_data.body: thanks, my assumption was that `\0` came from `read_file`. Adopted now based on your suggestions and added also the case for `\r` (old mac EOL) because we handle that case in code, so guess we should also test for it.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -567,6 +570,16 @@ end)
 describe('shell :!', function()
   before_each(clear)
 
+  it('preserves newlines (including CR) in binary-mode buffer #39424', function()
+    local fname = 'Xbinaryfile'
+    os.remove(fname)
+    eq(0, fn.writefile({ '\r\n' }, fname, 'b'))
+    command('edit ++bin ' .. fname)
+    command('%!cat')
+    command('w')
+    eq('\r\0', read_file(fname))

comment_regular_data.body:
The explanation is still really hard to follow. Can you confirm this rewrite:

```
Problem:
When `:!` writes shell output to a buffer, write_output() splits on `\r`, `\n`,
and `\r\n`, replacing the terminator byte with NUL. For a binary-mode buffer
this is wrong: `\r` should be preserved verbatim, not treated as a line
terminator. This wrong behavior causes a file like `\r\n` round-trips through
`:%!cat` to `\n`.

This was masked when 'shelltemp' was enabled, because output went through a temp
file and the regular file I/O path handled binary-mode correctly. Switching the
default to 'noshelltemp' exposed the bug, since output is now piped directly
into write_output().

Solution:
In `write_output()`, skip the `\r` and `\r\n` splits for a binary-mode buffer;
only split on `\n`.
```

> Now with `noshelltemp` set as default, we have to consider that conversions when reading or writing of `:!`.

even if it's not default, we still should handle that case?

>

> Problem:
> When `:!` writes shell output to a buffer, write_output() splits on `\r`, `\n`,
> and `\r\n`, replacing the terminator byte with NUL. For a binary-mode buffer

Yes this is a much better explanation. Thank you!

Yes

title: fix(shell): preserve CR when `:!` outputs to binary-mode buffer
